description:
You are working as a junior IT-support technician.  
    A senior colleague has asked you to create a compressed, version-controlled backup of an end-user’s trouble-ticket directory so it can be attached to a resolved-ticket report.

    Current ticket directory (already present on disk):
      /home/user/tickets/issue_20230815/
        ├── notes.txt
        ├── diagnostics.log
        └── screenshot.png

    What you must do:
    1. Create a gzip-compressed tar archive (tar + gzip) containing **the entire directory** issue_20230815 and everything inside it.
       • The resulting archive must be named:
           support_issue_20230815_backup.tar.gz
       • The archive must be stored in the directory:
           /home/user/backups
       • Inside the archive, the top-level folder must be issue_20230815 (i.e. when the archive is listed with “tar -tzf”, the first path fragment for every file must be “issue_20230815/…”).

    2. After successfully creating the archive, generate a **single-line** plain-text log file called:
           /home/user/backups/backup.log
       This file must contain **exactly** the following text (followed by a single trailing newline character):
           Backup completed: support_issue_20230815_backup.tar.gz

    Output/verification format details (what the automated tests will check):
      • /home/user/backups/support_issue_20230815_backup.tar.gz exists.
      • Listing that archive with “tar -tzf” prints exactly three entries, each prefixed by “issue_20230815/”:
            issue_20230815/notes.txt
            issue_20230815/diagnostics.log
            issue_20230815/screenshot.png
      • /home/user/backups/backup.log exists and its complete contents are:
            Backup completed: support_issue_20230815_backup.tar.gz\n
        (A single newline at the end — no extra spaces, blank lines, or additional text.)

    Constraints:
      • You have regular user privileges; do not attempt to use sudo.
      • Perform the task using normal command-line tools available on a standard Linux system.
      • Only one terminal command is necessary for each logical step, but feel free to use additional commands for verification or navigation while working.
truth:
Initial file system state before the agent starts:

    Directories:
      /home/user/tickets
      /home/user/tickets/issue_20230815
      /home/user/backups               (exists and is writable by the user)

    Files and their contents:

    1. /home/user/tickets/issue_20230815/notes.txt
       Content (exact, including trailing newline):
         User unable to connect to VPN.\n

    2. /home/user/tickets/issue_20230815/diagnostics.log
       Content (exact, including trailing newline):
         [2023-08-15 10:03] Ping to vpn.company.com failed.\n

    3. /home/user/tickets/issue_20230815/screenshot.png
       Binary placeholder; non-empty arbitrary binary data.

    Expected final state after successful task completion:

    • /home/user/backups/support_issue_20230815_backup.tar.gz exists.
      When extracted, its table of contents is precisely:
        issue_20230815/notes.txt
        issue_20230815/diagnostics.log
        issue_20230815/screenshot.png
      Each file’s data is identical to the originals.

    • /home/user/backups/backup.log exists with exact contents:
        Backup completed: support_issue_20230815_backup.tar.gz\n
name: task_000000_efe31ef4
